status: internal sales narrative draft, not buyer-ready final copy
positioning: Pre-Deployment Architectural Logic Stress-Testing and Constraint Mapping Framework for Agentic Workflows
one_sentence: SpecShift Architectural Logic Diagnostics helps teams stress-test proposed AI agent workflows before deployment by mapping where automated reasoning, evidence handling, scope boundaries, and escalation logic may break under synthetic edge-case review.
short_pitch: AI agents can sound confident while acting on weak evidence, stale records, unclear authority, or incomplete handoffs. SpecShift provides a pre-deployment diagnostic review that surfaces those failure patterns before broader rollout. The output is not a certification or runtime control system; it is a structured internal map of where the workflow appears stable under synthetic stress and where design guardrails may be needed.
buyer_problem: AI agents may treat forwarded documents, screenshots, summaries, or generated history as decision-grade evidence.
Agent workflows may exceed intended scope when approval boundaries are vague.
Multi-system workflows may act on stale records, mismatched states, or unverified completion claims.
Teams may lack clear escalation thresholds for when an AI workflow should pause, route, or require human review.
what_specshift_delivers: Adversarial Stress-Test Execution Report: Findings from simulated edge-case and failure-mode scenarios.
Limitation & Boundary Map: Structured inventory of where the AI workflow's reasoning may degrade under synthetic stress conditions.
Agentic Scope & Logic Alignment Review: Architecture-stage review of scope boundaries, data freshness assumptions, system-record consistency, and handoff logic.
Escalation & Human-in-the-Loop Routing Matrix: Proposed review thresholds and escalation paths for cases where the workflow should pause, route, or require human review.
pilot_offer: A bounded paid diagnostic engagement focused on one high-value AI agent workflow. SpecShift reviews the workflow against an internal adversarial stress-test suite, maps operational boundaries, identifies evidence-handling and state-alignment risks, and delivers architecture-stage review outputs for engineering, product, and risk teams.
safe_exclusions: Real-time production monitoring or runtime intervention.
Cybersecurity penetration testing or vulnerability scanning.
IAM administration, access-control enforcement, or HR decision tooling.
Guaranteeing zero hallucination, safety, or deployment readiness.
Replacing formal human audit, legal review, HR review, or regulated professional judgment.
pricing_posture: Priced as a professional diagnostic engagement rather than a SaaS subscription. The pilot fee covers structured stress-test execution, limitation mapping, and delivery of architecture-stage review outputs. This supports premium pilot pricing as a risk-reduction and design-review engagement without implying a software guarantee, compliance sign-off, or runtime control layer.
claim_boundary: This narrative stops fitting if it is used to imply certification, compliance sign-off, runtime monitoring, deployment readiness, or guaranteed AI safety. It should be used only to describe a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review engagement.
